summary: The implementation covers most requirements comprehensively. React Three Fiber is used for 3D rendering, player controls are implemented with WASD/mouse, shooting mechanics include muzzle flash and hit detection, the arena has obstacles and walls, enemies can be damaged and destroyed, game state is tracked both locally and via backend, HUD displays all required elements, and the visual aesthetic uses muted colors with atmospheric lighting. However, backend persistence is only partially implemented - the backend canister defines game state storage but there is no evidence that the frontend actually retrieves persisted state on session reload.

missing_requirements.requirement: Game state persists across sessions via backend
missing_requirements.reason: While backend functions exist to store game state, the frontend does not load persisted state when starting a new session. The useGameState hook initializes with hardcoded default values and only starts a new game via startGame mutation, which would overwrite any existing saved state rather than loading it.
